title: Floating Ring Magnets – Reasoning Tool
instructions_for_student: Look at the floating ring magnets on the vertical stand. Magnet X floats above Magnet Y. Answer each question by selecting the correct option. Watch the animation to check your reasoning.

question2Options: Remove the stand from the setup
Flip X so unlike poles face each other
Add a third magnet on top

metadata.subject: Science – Magnetism
metadata.section: 4.2 Poles of Magnet / 4.4 Attraction and Repulsion between Magnets
learning_objectives: Understand that like magnetic poles repel each other
Understand that unlike magnetic poles attract each other
Apply pole interaction rules to predict magnet behaviour
Reason through a cause-and-effect scenario using magnetism

agent_readme.description: An interactive two-question reasoning tool that simulates two ring magnets on a vertical stand. Magnet X floats above Magnet Y due to repulsion. Students answer why X floats and how to make X touch Y without pushing. Correct answers trigger physics animations (repulsion arrows, flip, snap-down).

quick_reference.question1Options: Array of 3 strings; option at index correctAnswer1 is the correct choice
quick_reference.question2Options: Array of 3 strings; option at index correctAnswer2 is the correct choice
quick_reference.correctAnswer1: Zero-based index of the correct answer for question 1 (default: 1)
quick_reference.correctAnswer2: Zero-based index of the correct answer for question 2 (default: 1)
quick_reference.magnetColor: Hex color for stand and magnet highlights (default: #4A4DC9)
quick_reference.standColor: Hex color for the vertical rod (default: #533086)

animations.q1_correct: Repulsion arrows animate between facing surfaces of X and Y; sparkles burst
animations.q2_correct_step2: Magnet X snaps down onto Y with elastic bounce; attraction arrows animate; sparkles burst
animations.completion: Full-width gradient completion banner appears with key-concept summary

question2Options: Remove the stand from the setup
Flip X so unlike poles face each other
Add a third magnet on top

question1Options: Same poles push away from each other
question2Options: Turn magnet X upside down so different poles face

question1Options: Earth's gravity is reversed at this location
Like poles of two magnets repel each other
The stand is made of a non-magnetic material
Air pressure is pushing X upward
question2Options: Heat magnet Y to reduce its magnetic strength
Flip magnet X so its unlike pole faces Y
Add a copper shield between the magnets
Increase the height of the stand

decision_tree.student_asks_why_X_floats: Use default config — correctAnswer1 points to 'like poles repel'
decision_tree.student_asks_how_to_make_contact: Use default config — correctAnswer2 points to 'flip X'
decision_tree.teacher_wants_harder_questions: Use four-option example with more distractors
decision_tree.custom_scenario: Provide your own question1Options / question2Options arrays, set correctAnswer indices
